title: Pagination, Filtering, Links, Rate Limits, Versioning, and Resumable Work · Browser and Node Client Systems · Narrowing Workshop
summary: An accessibility product team integrating a staging browser API must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for pagination, filtering, links, rate limits, versioning, and resumable work, producing resumable rate-aware API traversal. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch resumable rate-aware API traversal while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Traverse offset, page-number, cursor, and link pagination while detecting repeats, gaps, and termination. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Encode repeated parameters, arrays, dates, sorting, and filters according to the API contract without manual concatenation. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Coordinate server guidance, local quotas, concurrency, backoff, and caller priorities without synchronized bursts. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pagination Models
steps.instruction:
workshop case — accessible staging dashboard SDK follows cursor c5, repeated link c5, and rate limit 50/minute while records change between pages. Encode filters, detect loops, checkpoint stable identity, resume after failure, and avoid duplicates or gaps.
